description:
## Symptom

Post-arc CLIF goldens grew substantially: fib_tailrec went 353 → 768
lines (+11 CLIF functions, all any-key bodies), ast_eval_env emits 71
CLIF functions / 3975 lines. Debug-mode fixture_matrix run is dominated
by ast_eval_env JIT (49s) + AOT (51s); the rest of the matrix is 1-2s
per fixture.

## Root cause (one line)

`reachable_specs` in src/ir_typer.rs:2910-2918 seeds *every any-key in
the registry* as reachable. With ensure_any_keys (fz-aiz.3e) minting an
any-key for every reachable FnId, that blunt seed now drags an any-key
body into CLIF emission for every fn — even fns whose call sites all
resolve to narrow specs.

```rust
for (sid, fid, key) in spec_registry.iter() {
    let is_any_key = key.iter().all(|d| ...);
    if is_any_key {
        worklist.push(sid.0);
    }
}
```

The comment right above this block (lines 2898-2909) names the trade:
\"we don't model each source-of-entry channel precisely; we just
declare every any-key reachable.\" Pre-arc this was fine because direct-
only any-keys got dropped at the typer; the seed effectively only fired
for opaque-consumer fns. Post-arc the floor is total, so the seed now
fires for everything.

## The technique we have but aren't using

The annotation-driven dispatch arc (fz-aiz.3a-3c) gave the IR a direct
witness for which SpecId each call site dispatches to:

- `Term::Call.resolved_sid`
- `Term::TailCall.resolved_sid`
- `Term::CallClosure.callsite_sid` / `resolved_sid`
- `Term::TailCallClosure.callsite_sid` / `resolved_sid`
- `Term::Spawn.resolved_sid`
- `Cont.sid`
- `Prim::MakeClosure(_, _, Some(SpecId))` — body_sid

Every SpecId referenced from a reachable body is *exactly* the set we
need to seed beyond `main`. The any-key for a direct-only fn only
appears in this set if some site actually resolves to it (e.g., a
caller the typer couldn't narrow). If it doesn't appear → it's dead and
should be trap-stubbed.

## Proposed fix

Replace the blanket any-key seed with an annotation walk:

1. Seed `main`'s spec (already in place).
2. Seed `extra_seeds` (closure-target fns, spawn shims, scheduler hooks
   — channels the IR doesn't see). Already in place.
3. NEW: as the BFS pops a reachable SpecId, walk its FnIr terms and
   conts; each annotation's `resolved_sid` / `callsite_sid` / `body_sid`
   / `Cont.sid` becomes a worklist entry.
4. Delete the \"seed every any-key\" loop entirely.

The MakeClosure-target seed (lines 2938-2953, \"every spec of every
MakeClosure'd fn reachable\") may also tighten — but that's a separate
question; preserve it for now to keep the closure-lit dispatch story
unchanged.

## Expected outcome

- fib_tailrec: 22 → 11 CLIF functions, ~768 → ~353 lines (back to
  pre-arc shape).
- ast_eval_env: 71 → ~48 CLIF functions; debug-mode JIT/AOT runtime
  drops correspondingly (Cranelift debug-codegen-per-function dominates
  at this size).
- fixture_matrix debug runtime: 62s → ~20s.
- Release-mode and correctness invariants unchanged.
- The soundness floor (ensure_any_keys + total SpecRegistry::resolve)
  is preserved — the registry still carries every any-key; this change
  only affects which spec bodies CLIF emits.

## Acceptance

- Goldens regenerate and shrink as expected; CI still green.
- 787 unit + matrix tests still pass.
- Spec count in expected.specs unchanged (typer behavior preserved);
  CLIF function count drops to the set of SpecIds with annotation
  witnesses.
- Zero new warnings; clippy + fmt clean.

## See also

- fz-aiz.3e — ensure_any_keys; the source of the new any-keys.
- fz-aiz.3a–3c — annotation fields; the witness mechanism.
- src/ir_typer.rs:2872+ — reachable_specs.
title: fz-aiz.7: annotation-driven reachable_specs seeding — drop the any-key blanket seed
